behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names router module, WorkspaceRouterMiddleware export, InstanceRoutes, Instance.provide, InstanceBootstrap, directory query param, x-opencode-directory header, worktree adaptor fetch throwing, old middleware removal, and server.ts import — all assertions in tests are derivable.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes what's broken and expected behavior (branches, consolidation) without giving specific code diffs or line changes.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests check for AST patterns (Instance.provide calls, InstanceBootstrap, query('directory'), 'x-opencode-directory' literal, adaptor.fetch) which any reasonable implementation following the spec would include.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests use bun to import modules and parse TypeScript AST via the typescript compiler, actually loading and analyzing code structure rather than grep.

test_not_tautological.reason: Tests require ≥2 Instance.provide calls with InstanceBootstrap, removal from instance.ts, deletion of old file, and specific header/query literals — a stub can't satisfy these.

behavior_in_tests.reason: Each required behavior in instruction (single entry point, directory resolution, three branches, delegation, cleanup) has a corresponding test.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests grouped under clear section headers 'Fail-to-pass' and 'Pass-to-pass' with descriptive names and docstrings.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is code refactoring, not structured data production.

no_typos.reason: File paths and identifiers are consistent throughout instruction and tests.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names the files to investigate (appropriate as localization is part of setup), but doesn't name specific lines to change.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no config edits involved.
